summary: The route_slug_alias.route_id foreign key created in evolution 344 is not mirrored with a foreignKey definition in the Slick model, violating the 'Mirror each in the Slick model' rule.
failure_scenario: Repo CLAUDE.md (Database & evolutions): 'Mirror each in the Slick model so schema and code agree: ... foreignKey(...) an FK.' 344.sql:37 declares route_id INT NOT NULL REFERENCES route(route_id), but RouteSlugAliasTableDef (13-19) has no foreignKey def (contrast MissionTableDef in the same PR, which mirrors its new mission_user_route_id_fkey).

summary: The UNIQUE index on route.slug created in evolution 344 is not mirrored with an index(..., unique = true) in the Slick model.
failure_scenario: Repo CLAUDE.md: 'Mirror each in the Slick model ... index(..., unique = true) a UNIQUE.' 344.sql:44 runs CREATE UNIQUE INDEX route_slug_idx ON route (slug); but RouteTableDef declares slug (line 71) with no index(...) anywhere (66-84) — the same PR's RouteStreetTableDef shows the expected pattern for its UNIQUE (route_id, position).

summary: Type set with hand-assembled font shorthands on raw font variables instead of composite --text-* tokens (lines 148 and 110).
failure_scenario: Repo CLAUDE.md: 'Set type with a composite --text-* token, not the raw font variables... keep the token and override just that property.' Line 148 writes font: 700 24px/30px var(--font-primary) though --text-h3-bold is 700 24px/28px var(--font-primary) — the exact sanctioned override case; line 110 hand-assembles font: 700 20px/24px var(--font-accent). Both also drop the var(--ui-scale) factor tokens bake in.

summary: Mapbox static-map thumbnail URL template hardcoded verbatim in both frontend JS (1005-1006) and Twirl view (dashboard.scala.html:68-69), violating the centralize-literals rule.
failure_scenario: Repo CLAUDE.md (Backend is the source of truth): 'A hardcoded frontend copy silently drifts... Centralize + justify — define it in one place and comment why it isn't sourced from the backend.' Two independent copies of the same style-id/color/size literal with no single source; a change in one silently desyncs the other's thumbnails.

summary: The new route_street.position column gets no CHECK constraint for its non-negative bounded domain.
failure_scenario: Repo CLAUDE.md: 'Give every table its full set of constraints... a CHECK for a bounded domain (a severity 1-3, a non-negative count...).' 344.sql:11 adds position INT (NOT NULL at 19) — a non-negative ordinal backfilled as rn-1 and written from zipWithIndex — with no CHECK (position >= 0); only the app enforces the domain.
